{"query":"Notre-Dame de Paris spire copper statues returned date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1734,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.friendsofnotredamedeparis.org/notre-dame-cathedrals-2025-reconstruction-progress-updates/","title":"Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates | Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris","content":"Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates 12 Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates 13 Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates 14 Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates 15 Notre-Dame Cathedral's 2025 Reconstruction Progress Updates 16
In June and July 2025, it is the turn of the 16 statues of the apostles to return to their place at the foot of the spire, after their restoration in the workshop."},{"url":"https://thegoodnewsroom.org/notre-dame-prepares-to-reopen-towers-tour-with-return-of-famed-statues-to-rooftop/","title":"Notre Dame Prepares To Reopen Towers' Tour With Return of Famed Statues to Rooftop - The Good Newsroom","content":"PARIS (OSV News) — Symbolic lifting of the statues surrounding the spire of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris marked the beginning of one of the last phases of the roof reconstruction before the towers of the Parisian icon will reopen to the public on September 20.
It bears the features of Viollet-le-Duc himself, and its gaze is turned toward the spire, unlike the others, which are turned toward Paris.
Another project will involve replacing the discreet, non-figurative stained-glass windows that Viollet-le-Duc installed in six side chapels on the south side of the cathedral — with contemporary figurative stained-glass windows on the theme of Pentecost, a project expected to be completed by December 2026.
“I see many people experiencing the same thing,” Father Ribadeau Dumas said.
Caroline de Sury writes for OSV News from Paris."},{"url":"https://news.artnet.com/art-world/notre-dame-cathedral-spire-statues-return-2660990","title":"Notre-Dame Welcomes Back Its Beloved Spire Statues","content":"Notre-Dame hopes to return all of the statues by the end of July.
On June 23, after six years of restoration work and following a blessing from the archbishop of Paris, Laurent Ulrich, the first of 16 larger-than-life copper statues was returned to its perch atop Notre-Dame’s spire, the latest step in the cathedral’s roughly $900 million rebuild.
“In a few weeks, we will see the spire completely stripped of scaffolding and the statues at its base will once again be clearly visible,” the president of Rebuilding Notre-Dame, the public institution responsible for the restoration, said in a statement."},{"url":"https://curatorial.ro/en/art/giant-statues-return-to-the-spire-of-notre-dame-cathedral-photo-gallery/","title":"Giant statues return to the spire of Notre Dame Cathedral/ photo gallery - Curatorial","content":"Sixteen giant statues will be re-erected on Notre Dame’s spire as part of the latest stage of the cathedral’s €700m (£600m) reconstruction after the devastating fire in 2019.
The statues were designed by Eugène Viollet-le-Duc for the 19th-century remodeling of Notre Dame and created by sculptor Adolphe Victor Geoffroy-Dechaume.
Installed in 1861, the apostles are each 3.4 meters high and are arranged in four groups around the 96-meter spire, which was added by Viollet-le-Duc in 1858.
Mark, believed to date from World War II."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spire_of_Notre-Dame_de_Paris","title":"Spire of Notre-Dame de Paris - Wikipedia","content":"Restored date — 18 June 1859, 16 December 2023
Work to construct a third one started in 2022, and was completed when the new copper rooster wind vane was placed on top of the new spire on 16 December 2023, and the third spire was unveiled on 13 February 2024.
View of the île de la Cité featuring the Notre-Dame cathedral, by Nicolas-Jean-Baptiste Raguenet (1752)
On 16 December 2023 the new gold copper spire rooster, which had been blessed by Archbishop of Paris Laurent Ulrich, was put on the new spire to complete it."},{"url":"https://parisjetaime.com/eng/article/notre-dame-the-current-situation-a741","title":"Notre-Dame de Paris - Reopening - Opening to the public","content":"May 20, 2026 — statues from the spire will return, on 9 May 2026: extended opening hours until 10pm. On 16 December 2023 the new gold copper spire rooster, It was revealed on 13 February 2024.","score":0.902687,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.friendsofnotredamedeparis.org/notre-dame-cathedral-statues","title":"Big update: Notre-Dame Cathedral Statues Removed","content":"# Notre-Dame Cathedral Statues Removed for Restoration. Picture of Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris. ## Notre-Dame Cathedral Statues were safely and successfully removed from the cathedral’s spire! April 11, 2019 marked a big occasion: the huge Notre-Dame Cathedral statues that adorn the spire were removed. The statues consist of a copper sheathing attached to an internal iron structure. The removal of Notre-Dame Cathedral statues, which have been guarding Paris since 1861, is the symbolic concretization of the commitment to the great works in the coming years. ## The history of the spire and its statues. The 16 copper Notre-Dame Cathedral statues will go to a workshop in Périgueux in southwestern France where they will be examined by experts. The statues will stay in the cathedral until the end of the restoration of the spire. Friends of Notre-Dame de Paris is grateful to these dedicated supporters and looks forward to providing updates on the statues’ journey to restoration.","score":0.84707206,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/jun/23/giant-statues-of-saints-to-crown-latest-step-in-600m-restoration-of-notre-dame","title":"Giant statues to return to Notre Dame’s spire in latest stage of restoration | Notre Dame | The Guardian","content":"They are being returned to the cathedral after a six-year absence. # Giant statues to return to Notre Dame’s spire in latest stage of restoration. Copper-coated figures will be hoisted on to cathedral’s reconstructed spire after devastating blaze of 2019. Sixteen giant statues are to be hoisted back on to the spire of Notre Dame in the latest step of the cathedral’s €700m (£600m) reconstruction after the devastating fire of 2019. On Monday evening, after a blessing from the archbishop of Paris, Laurent Ulrich, the statue of Saint Thomas will be returned to the reconstructed spire. The depictions of the 11 other apostles and four evangelists will be put back “in stages” according to the Notre Dame team. All the statues look out over Paris except for that of Saint Thomas, the patron saint of architects, who looks toward the spire and is said to be modelled on Viollet-le-Duc. The statues have undergone a complete renewal, with damaged parts replaced and corrosion removed, returning them to their original dark brown colour.","score":0.7935067,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://news.artnet.com/art-world/notre-dame-cathedral-spire-statues-return-2660990","title":"Notre-Dame Welcomes Back Its Beloved Spire Statues","content":"Notre-Dame hopes to return all of the statues by the end of July.
